2606.04806.title: NoRA: Evaluating Grounded Reasonableness in Visual First-person Normative Action Reasoning
2606.04806.paper_summary: LLMs and agentic systems are increasingly deployed in social environments, making normative competence critical for safe and appropriate behavior. However, existing approaches either assess normative judgment in text alone or reduce it to choosing among a fixed set of candidate actions. We argue both are insufficient. In practice, agents are never handed a menu of options; they must identify a reasonable action from scratch, grounded in visible facts and supported by inspectable reasons. We introduce NoRA, a visual first-person video benchmark that requires models to generate candidate next actions and justify each through an explicit fact-reason-action support graph. The benchmark comprises 1,420 annotated video clips, including HumanGold-190 and LLMSilver-1230 splits. Each instance is evaluated through action alignment, factual grounding, and support binding, aggregated into a single grounded reasonableness score. We benchmark 12 multimodal systems under direct, deliberate, and structured prompting regimes, finding that current VLMs frequently recover plausible actions and relevant scene facts, but consistently struggle to construct the full reasonable action space and bind selected actions to the correct local support. NoRA makes this gap measurable, shifting the evaluation question from whether a model can pick an action to whether it can justify an appropriate action for the right visible reasons.
2606.04806.paper_summary_zh: LLMs 和代理系統越來越多地在社會環境中部署，使得規範能力對於安全和適當的行為至關重要。<lb/>然而，現有的方法要麼僅在文本中評估規範判斷，要麼將其簡化為在固定候選行動中進行選擇。我們認為這兩者都不夠充分。<lb/>在實踐中，代理從來不會被提供一個選項菜單；他們必須從頭開始識別出合理的行動，這基於可見的事實並由可檢查的理由支持。<lb/>我們介紹了 NoRA，一個視覺第一人稱視頻基準，要求模型生成候選下一步行動並通過明確的事實-理由-行動支持圖來為每個行動提供理由。<lb/>該基準包含 1,420 個註釋視頻片段，包括 HumanGold-190 和 LLMSilver-1230 的拆分。<lb/>每個實例通過行動對齊、事實基礎和支持綁定進行評估，最終匯總為一個單一的基礎合理性分數。<lb/>我們在直接、故意和結構化的提示體系下對 12 個多模態系統進行基準測試，發現當前的 VLMs 經常能夠恢復合理的行動和相關的場景事實，但始終難以構建完整的合理行動空間並將所選行動綁定到正確的本地支持上。<lb/>NoRA 使這一差距可測量，將評估問題的重點從模型是否能選擇行動轉變為它是否能為正確的可見理由辯護一個適當的行動。
2606.04806.author: Sichao Li et.al.
2606.04806.authors: Sichao Li, Sai Ma, Daniel Kilov, Secil Yanik Guyot, Zhuang Li, Seth Lazar
